method_notes: Built from impact/gc_ch00..42.impact.json (43 files, complete). Final verdicts are defense-adjusted: original verification verdict overridden by defense.revised_verdict where defense result was defense_succeeded or verdict_downgraded. Corpus verdict totals derive from manifest.json per-chapter verify counts plus the 34 defense adjustments observed in the impact files; manifest bookkeeping carries a small internal variance (extract total 4443 vs verify-sum 4440, chapters 21 and 34), flagged here rather than silently patched. Impact metrics copied verbatim from impact files.

notable.GC-14-032: contradicted -> supported (Bishop of Durham buying Tyndale Bibles: defense vindicated the account)
notable.GC-04-036: contradicted -> anachronistic (Sabbath as cause of Waldensian separation: traced to Andrews/Wylie)
notable.GC-15-152: misquotation -> anachronistic (Voltaire 'one man' quote: period-inherited attribution)
notable.GC-15-155: misquotation -> anachronistic ('Bible is an anvil' attribution)
notable.GC-37-022: misquotation -> unverifiable (defense located the quote tradition)

claim_type_distribution_manifest.note: sums of manifest.json by_type; total 4456 vs 4443 extracted, manifest-internal variance ~0.3%

key_metrics.quote_layer: of 1283 attributed_quote claims, zero misquotation verdicts survived defense (2 downgraded to anachronistic in ch15, 1 reversed in ch37, 1 reversed in ch04)

per_chapter.title: The Bible And The French Revolution

clusters.name: sabbath_continuity_successionism
clusters.description: Unbroken chain of seventh-day Sabbath keepers from apostles through the Middle Ages (Waldenses, Culdees, Armenians, hidden remnant). Traced to J.N. Andrews, History of the Sabbath (1873), ch. 'Traces of the Sabbath During the Dark Ages', and Wylie/Muston. Rejected by modern scholarship incl. Ball, The Seventh-Day Men (Clarendon 1994).

clusters.name: sunday_change_by_rome
clusters.description: Rome as originator of Sunday observance and author of the Sabbath-to-Sunday change. Live scholarly dispute (Bacchiocchi vs Rordorf/Carson); papal-decree framing anachronistic.

clusters.name: rome_and_the_bible
clusters.description: Blanket claims that Rome prohibited/withheld the Bible from the people for centuries (and still does, in 1911 present tense). Modern scholarship documents restricted vernacular-translation policy after 1229/Trent, not a total ban; anachronistic or disputed depending on formulation.

clusters.name: chronology_1844
clusters.description: The 457 BC / AD 27 / AD 31 / AD 34 / 1844 chain. Historically accurate as a report of Millerite reckoning; as objective chronology it rests on the contested 457-vs-458 BC anchor (Parker & Dubberstein: 458) and on the year-day principle rejected by mainstream Daniel scholarship (Antiochus IV reading).
source_lineage: S. Bliss, Memoirs of William Miller

clusters.name: scheme_538_1798
clusters.description: 1260-year papal supremacy dated AD 538-1798. Historicist construct (Uriah Smith lineage); only the 1798 terminus (capture of Pius VI) is an independently anchored event.

clusters.name: waldensian_antiquity_and_mission
clusters.description: Waldensian apostolic antiquity, thousand-year Alpine continuity, missionary-school idealizations, and the Netherlands/Bohemia continuity bridges. Wylie/Muston/Perrin tradition built on misdated documents (Nobla Leyczon); modern dating starts with Waldo c.1173.

clusters.name: celtic_church_romanticism
clusters.description: Iona/Culdee narrative: early British Christianity independent of Rome, Sabbath-keeping, destroyed by papal aggression; Columba's Scotland lineage. 19th-century Protestant romanticism.

clusters.name: reformation_embellishments
clusters.description: Dramatized episodes and hero/villain sharpening inherited from D'Aubigne and Wylie: Scala Sancta voice, Tetzel's crimes and sayings, Muenzer's motives, Coburg hymn, dramatic execution of Louis XVI, Briconnet's stake choice, Anne of Bohemia, half-of-England Lollardy, Hussite century-prophecy. Event cores usually solid; the coloring is period-inherited.

clusters.name: surviving_contradicted
clusters.description: The only two accusatory verdicts of the 'contradicted' class that survived the defense pass. Both are isolated slips, neither load-bearing.

clusters.name: inflated_statistics
clusters.description: Casualty and scale figures taken from period sources at their high end: Josephus's million at Jerusalem, 70,000 St. Bartholomew, Lisbon 60k/90k, 'millions of martyrs', Wesley's half-million.

clusters.name: ussher_chronology_frame
clusters.description: Ussher-style biblical chronology as narrative frame: 6000 years, 2500 years without written revelation, 1600 years of Bible composition, 4000-year sacrificial system, Joel 2500 years before 1780.

clusters.name: immortality_of_soul_genealogy
clusters.description: Natural immortality as pagan doctrine channelled through Rome into Christendom; linked to spiritualism vulnerability. Simplified genealogy of a complex patristic development; genuine scholarly debate on 'conditionalism' history.

clusters.name: hebrews_pauline_authorship
clusters.description: Paul named as author of Hebrews; standard 19th-century attribution, abandoned by modern scholarship.

clusters.name: jerusalem_ad70
clusters.description: AD 70 narrative details: zero Christian casualties (Pella tradition), Titus's horror and order to spare the temple, Passover millions. Rests on Eusebius/Josephus read through Milman's History of the Jews.

clusters.name: french_revolution_rev11
clusters.description: French Revolution as fulfilment of Revelation 11: 3.5-year suppression/restoration interval engineered to match prophecy; 'Crush the Wretch' aimed at Christ; Voltaire quotes. The 1793-1797 'exact fulfilment' claim conflicts with the law of 21 Feb 1795.

clusters.name: millerite_movement_framing
clusters.description: The awakening as simultaneous independent discovery ('solely by the study of the Scriptures'), Litch's 1840 Ottoman prediction, Lisbon/dark-day signs. Individual events real; the independence/sign framing is the contested layer.

strongest_defenses.note: contradicted -> supported: the Bishop of Durham Bible-buying episode was vindicated
misquotation withdrawn after the defense located the quotation tradition
contradicted -> anachronistic: traced to Andrews/Wylie, removing the fabrication charge
misquotation -> anachronistic: Voltaire attribution standard in 19th-c. literature
defense honestly tested the anachronism excuse and rejected it: period sources (Wellcome 1874, Irving 1827) spell 'Ben-Ezra' correctly, so the slip is White's own

defense_pass_highlights.systemic_observation: The defense pass moved every misquotation off the books (4 -> 0) and reduced contradicted from 4 to 2; 96.9% of surviving accusatory verdicts are anachronistic, i.e. inherited from the era's historiography rather than invented.
